openmeter/watermill/driver/kafka/metrics.purpose: Bridges go-metrics (used by Sarama/Kafka clients) to OpenTelemetry by wrapping go-metrics types so every metric mutation is forwarded to an otelmetric.Meter. Enables Kafka client telemetry to appear in the OpenTelemetry pipeline without changing Sarama internals.

patterns.name: Wrap-and-delegate metric types
patterns.description: Each wrapped type (wrappedMeter, wrappedCounter, wrappedGauge, wrappedGaugeFloat64, wrappedHistogram) embeds the original go-metrics interface and overrides only mutation methods (Mark, Inc, Dec, Update, Record) to call both the OTel instrument and the underlying go-metrics method.
patterns.example: type wrappedCounter struct { metrics.Counter; otelMeter otelmetric.Int64UpDownCounter; attributes attribute.Set } — Inc calls otelMeter.Add then Counter.Inc

patterns.name: Registry delegation via embedding
patterns.description: registry embeds metrics.Registry for all read/list operations and only overrides Register and GetOrRegister to intercept new metric registrations and wrap them.
patterns.example: type registry struct { metrics.Registry; mu sync.Mutex; meticMeter otelmetric.Meter; ... }

patterns.name: TransformMetricsNameToOtel for name mapping and drop filtering
patterns.description: All metric names pass through NameTransformFn before OTel instrument creation. Returning Drop:true bypasses OTel wrapping and returns the raw go-metrics type unchanged.
patterns.example: transfomedMetric := r.nameTransformFn(name); if transfomedMetric.Drop { return def, nil }

patterns.name: Mutex-guarded registration
patterns.description: GetOrRegister and Register acquire r.mu before checking or writing to the embedded Registry, preventing races on concurrent Sarama metric registration.
patterns.example: r.mu.Lock(); defer r.mu.Unlock() at the top of both GetOrRegister and Register

patterns.name: Reflect-based metric factory unwrapping
patterns.description: def passed to getWrappedMeter may be a zero-arg function returning the metric (Sarama pattern). reflect.ValueOf(def).Kind() == reflect.Func triggers a call to extract the actual metric before type-switching.
patterns.example: if v := reflect.ValueOf(def); v.Kind() == reflect.Func { def = v.Call(nil)[0].Interface() }

patterns.name: context.Background() for OTel calls (documented limitation)
patterns.description: All OTel instrument calls use context.Background() because go-metrics mutation methods carry no context parameter. This is an accepted limitation documented in README.md.

key_file_guides.role: Entire package implementation: Registry constructor, wrapped metric structs, and their OTel-forwarding mutation methods.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Adding a new go-metrics type requires: (1) a new wrapped struct embedding that type, (2) a new case in getWrappedMeter's type switch, (3) override of every mutation method. Missing any method leaves silent gaps where OTel receives no data.

key_file_guides.role: Documents the known limitations: context is always context.Background() and metric registration errors are only logged, never propagated to callers.
key_file_guides.watch_for: If OTel metric registration errors are silently swallowed by errorHandler, callers see no panic but metrics are missing entirely.

anti_patterns: Adding a new metric type without implementing ALL mutation methods on the wrapper — partial delegation silently drops OTel events.
Calling r.Registry.Register directly inside getWrappedMeter instead of returning the wrapped value — double-registration under the same name will occur.
Removing the reflect-based factory unwrapping — Sarama passes metric factories as zero-arg functions, not direct metric values; removing the reflect call breaks those registrations.
Initialising NewRegistry without a MetricMeter — constructor returns an error; callers must check it or OTel forwarding is entirely absent.
Using a real propagated context instead of context.Background() in wrapped methods — go-metrics interfaces have no context parameter so callers cannot propagate one.

decisions.decision: Embed metrics.Registry rather than re-implementing it
decisions.rationale: All read/iteration/unregister operations on the registry are unchanged; only the write path (Register, GetOrRegister) needs interception to wrap new metrics, minimising the surface area of this adapter.

decisions.decision: TransformMetricsNameToOtel as an injectable function with Drop support
decisions.rationale: Sarama emits dozens of internal metrics; callers need to filter noise and rename metrics to match OTel naming conventions without forking this package.
go-metrics mutation methods (Mark, Inc, Update) carry no context; propagating caller context is structurally impossible without changing the go-metrics interface, which would require forking Sarama.

code_examples.scenario: Register a custom go-metrics registry that forwards to OTel, filtering noise metrics via NameTransformFn
